title: Peeling Back the Texas Pro-Life Façade
author: James Silberman
orientation: Silberman dissects the 2019 Texas Born-Alive Infant Protection Act (HB16) as a piece of political theater used by Rep. Jeff Leach and Gov. Greg Abbott to mask their suppression of the Abolition of Abortion in Texas Act (HB896). He argues that the incrementalist 'pro-life victories' churn is a deliberate strategy to keep the pro-life voting bloc dependent on politicians who will never actually abolish abortion.
audiences: Christian pastor discipling on civic engagement

questions.question: What does HB16 actually change about the legal protection of babies who survive abortion attempts?
questions.answer: Despite Leach and Abbott's claim that HB16 grants 'full protection of the law,' the only substantive change is upgrading the penalty for letting such a baby die from a misdemeanor to a third-degree felony. That is far short of treating the killing as murder, which is what equal protection would require.
questions.quote: On September 1st, the penalty for allowing a baby born alive to die on the table will become a third-degree felony rather than a misdemeanor. The change is miniscule, and babies who survive abortions are not given anywhere near full protection of the laws against murder like they should be.

questions.question: Why does Silberman call the pro-life legislative cycle a 'game'?
questions.answer: He argues the pro-life lobby manufactures a perpetual stream of incremental bills, each spun as a victory, so that voters remain emotionally satisfied and politically dependent on the same politicians who refuse to abolish abortion. Because the supply of marginal bills is essentially unlimited, the cycle can run forever if voters never demand more.
questions.quote: This is the game pro-life politicians play to keep the pro-life voting bloc dependent on them. That's all HB16 is – an unjust law that has absolutely no effect on anything except to give pro-life politicians and establishment lobbyists something to show pro-life voters. They will continue to play this game in perpetuity if we let them because there's an infinite number of worthless and/or unjust bills that could be spun as pro-life victories.

questions.question: How does the Oklahoma episode with Treat and Smalley illustrate the same dynamic?
questions.answer: After Senators Greg Treat and Jason Smalley killed the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act and faced flyers, billboards, and a flood of calls, they passed three pro-life bills that accomplish nothing. Because pro-life voters still treated the bills as wins, the legislators were able to weather the backlash and keep their seats.
questions.quote: In response, they passed three pro-life bills which accomplish nothing and saved their careers because people still see these pro-life bills as victories, even when these bills are transparently used as the excuse not to abolish abortion.

questions.question: What role did Jeff Leach play in the fate of HB896?
questions.answer: Leach is named as the gatekeeper who blocked the Abolition of Abortion in Texas Act from receiving a committee vote, and HB16 functions as his political alibi. Authoring and passing HB16 gives him a 'pro-life' credit to wave at constituents while obscuring his role in killing the bill that would have ended abortion in Texas.
questions.quote: Jeff Leach is the man responsible for preventing the Abolition of abortion in Texas Act (TX HB896) from getting a committee vote. For the sake of his career, he needs to distract people from the fact that he killed HB896 with the fact that he authored and helped pass HB16.

questions.question: What promise did Abbott make to Jeremiah Thomas, and how did he handle it?
questions.audience: Christian pastor discipling on civic engagement
questions.answer: In June 2018, Abbott personally promised dying 16-year-old Jeremiah Thomas that he would work for abortion's abolition in the 2019 session. After receiving more than 70,000 petitions reminding him of that pledge before his state of the state address, Abbott declined to mention abolition and, according to a source close to him, did nothing to support HB896 because he did not actually support it.
questions.quote: In June of 2018, Abbott promised dying 16-year-old Jeremiah Thomas that he would work for abortion's abolition in the 2019 session. In February, Abbott's office was delivered more than 70,000 petitions by Jeremiah's family and friends reminding him to keep his promise to Jeremiah.

questions.question: What hypothetical legislative escalation does Silberman predict, and what is its rhetorical purpose?
questions.answer: He sketches a session-by-session pattern in which the same politicians upgrade the born-alive penalty from third-degree to second-degree to first-degree felony before finally writing equal protection in 2025. The point is to expose how the inadequacy of HB16 is itself the product line: each future 'fix' becomes another marketable victory rather than the actual abolition that could have been enacted in the first place.
questions.quote: It wouldn't be surprising if next session (which for Texas is in 2021), a pro-life politician points out the injustice of this born-alive act and introduces a bill to make murdering a baby born alive after an abortion attempt a second-degree felony. In 2023, the pro-life lobby will call that law unjust and pass a law making it a first degree felony, and then in 2025, they'll actually write a law providing equal protection of the law against murder for babies who survive botched abortions.

questions.question: According to Silberman, what specifically must voters do for abortion to actually be abolished?
questions.answer: He says abolition will not happen until voters refuse to accept the staged 'victories' that politicians and lobbyists use as cover and instead demand total and immediate abolition. The mechanism is moral and political: as long as voters celebrate marginal bills, the politicians have no incentive to do more.
questions.quote: When we call out the game for what it is, reject the worthless "pro-life victories" that the politicians and lobbyists use as their excuse not to abolish abortion, and demand abortion's total and immediate abolition, abortion will be abolished. But not until then.

related.title: Pro-Life Texas State Rep. Jeff Leach Delivers Death Sentence to 110,000 Preborn Human Beings
related.reason: The companion piece naming Leach as the killer of HB896, which is the underlying betrayal HB16 is designed to cover up.

related.title: 110,000 Lives Hang In the Balance As Abolition of Abortion in Texas Act Gets April 8th Hearing
related.reason: Sets the stakes of the very bill (TX HB896) that Leach and Abbott declined to advance.

related.title: Supreme Court Idolatry at Texas Alliance for Life
related.reason: Profiles Joe Pojman's Texas Alliance for Life, one of the lobbying outfits behind the kind of incrementalist posture Silberman is criticizing.

related.title: Don't Fall For Texas Right to Life's Faux Abolitionism
related.reason: Continues the Texas pro-life critique by exposing another major Texas lobby attempting to co-opt abolitionist language while opposing abolition.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Provides the Oklahoma backdrop, including Treat and Smalley, that Silberman cites as the parallel to what Leach and Abbott pulled in Texas.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Deepens the case study on Greg Treat that this article references when describing how 'pro-life' bills were used to weather backlash.

related.title: Former ERLC Policy Director Matt Hawkins Glad SBC Squashed Abolition "Like a Bug"
related.reason: Extends the pattern beyond legislators to the institutional pro-life establishment that polices and suppresses abolition efforts.
